_readme: Claims manifest. Every quantitative claim this project makes in user-facing
text, paired with the measurement that regenerates it from the tree.
Checked by scripts/claims_check.py, which is wired into CI and pre-push.
Adding a claim to any surface without adding a row here is the failure this
file exists to prevent; the checker cannot detect a claim it was never told
about, so a row is part of writing the claim, not a follow-up to it.
kind:
exact  -- claimed value must equal the measured value
floor  -- claimed value must be <= the measured value. LIMITATION: a floor
claim can go silently false in the other direction. A hardcoded
'coverage 86%+' badge stays green when coverage falls to 80%,
because 86 <= 80 is the only thing that would fail and it does
not. Floor rows detect overstatement, never decay. The only real
fix is a dynamic badge; see FOLLOWUPS.md item 26.
surface_kind -- what is actually checked, not where the surface lives:
repo      -- file in this repository. Read on every run; 'locator' must
appear in it verbatim, so a reworded claim fails loudly
instead of leaving its row checking nothing; value compared
against measurement.
fetched   -- published elsewhere but retrievable over HTTP. Fetched on
every run; 'locator' must appear verbatim in the fetched
body; value compared against measurement. A fetch failure is
a violation, never a skip.
external  -- neither retrievable nor correctable from a commit. Only the
recorded value is compared, so the row proves this manifest
is self-consistent and proves NOTHING about the surface. That
is a permanent property of the kind, so every external row
must carry 'unverifiable_reason' and is printed on every run.
The previous wording said external meant 'unreachable from CI'. That was
false for five of the seven rows it covered -- the profile README is a
public raw URL -- and it is what made a hole look like a design decision.
It also said 'updating the row and editing the surface are one act', which
described a discipline nothing enforced.
unverifiable_reason vs deferred.reason -- two different properties:
unverifiable_reason  -- why this surface can never be read by the gate.
Permanent, definitional to surface_kind external.
deferred             -- this row currently DISAGREES and cannot be fixed
from a commit. Temporary, and self-cancelling: the
gate fails once a deferred row agrees again.
A row may need both. Requiring a deferral on every external row would be
a contradiction -- an external row that agrees would then have no legal
state, because deferred-and-agreeing is itself a violation.
Accepted dependency: 'fetched' rows make the surface's host a gating
dependency of every push and every CI run here. If
github.com/hassanzaibhay/hassanzaibhay is renamed, made private, or changes
default branch, this gate fails until the affected rows are edited. That is
the correct consequence of treating a fetch failure as a violation, and it
is recorded here so it is not diagnosed from scratch during an outage.

claims.locator: Running all 7 analyzers over captured queries
CI enforces a minimum of **85% coverage**
CI enforces a minimum of 85% coverage

claims.unverifiable_reason: Discourse post bodies are not served as plain text at a stable URL, and the post can only be edited by its author while signed in. The gate can neither read nor correct it.

deferred.reason: The post has said 434 since 2026-03-16 and cannot be corrected from a commit, so failing the gate on it would make every build red for a surface no commit can fix. (Why it cannot be checked at all is the separate unverifiable_reason above; this field is only about the current disagreement.)
deferred.action: Edit post 117670 during the S14 external-surfaces pass, then delete this deferral. The gate fails if the row is still marked deferred once it agrees with the tree, so the deferral cannot outlive the fix.

claims.unverifiable_reason: Same surface and same limits as forum-post-test-count: not readable by the gate, not editable from a commit. PREDICTED FAILURE: this row agrees today (7 == 7) and will become a hard violation at S11, when the eighth analyzer takes measure_analyzer_count() to 8. That is the gate working, not a broken build. Remedy at that point: edit forum post 117670, or add a deferral with a named action if it cannot be edited in time.

claims.note: Makes 'the backlog is shrinking' measured rather than asserted. open = headings - tombstones - entries carrying a '- **Resolved:**' line. A tombstone cannot inflate it (heading title contains 'merged into entry', excluded). A reserved number cannot inflate it either (25 is reserved for the phase-1 disposition and has no heading at all). '- **Resolved (partial):**' does NOT count as resolved, which is why entry 12 stays open. FOLLOWUPS.md is deliberately absent from gated_files: it records dated measurements by design and would fight the prose rules.

_note: Rules that reject a shape of claim rather than check a number.
Applied to the same explicit file list the docs truth sweep gates.

build_time.reason: Build durations are not properties of the tree. The same `mkdocs build --strict` produced 4.14s and 7.89s on different runs; quoting either as a fact makes the artifact wrong on the next machine.

dated_status.reason: A dated assertion about something's CURRENT status rots without anyone touching the file. comparison.md said fetch modes were 'unreleased as of 2026-07-14', which becomes false when Django 6.1 ships. Dated PROVENANCE claims are the opposite -- 'comparisons current as of <date>' records when a comparison was made and stays true permanently -- so they are exempt.
dated_status.limitation: This rule fires only on ISO-dated lines, so it catches a strict subset of what it is aimed at: 'Django 6.1 is unreleased' rots identically and is not caught, and the cheapest route to green is therefore to delete the date, which makes the claim less auditable. Tightening it to undated status words was measured against the tree and produced 6 false positives (CONTRIBUTING.md:80,107,109,110,111,115, all of them the literal '[Unreleased]' changelog heading in changelog-process prose) and 0 true positives. A date is the only reliable machine-detectable signal that a sentence asserts current status rather than describing a process. The honest mitigation for the undated case is review, not detection.
dated_status.provenance_matching: A marker exempts a date only when it INTRODUCES that date -- marker, optional connector, separators, then the date. It is deliberately not a distance window: a character count is a tunable with no principle behind it. The previous whole-line substring test exempted any line merely containing 'measured' anywhere, including in an unrelated clause. If a real provenance line fails to match, extend the connector group in _provenance_pattern.

_gated_files_note: Explicit list, deliberately not a repo-root glob: SPEC.md and CLAUDE.md are
gitignored by decision (D-B2), and a glob would gate content that is not in
the repository at all. CHANGELOG.md is excluded from the prose rules because
its historical entries record what was true at the time of each release --
the same permanence argument that exempts dated-provenance claims.
